User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the BookYourBrands CRM.

profilePic.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project in the BookYourBrands CRM.

createdAt.description: Date and time the project was created.

status.description: Current status of the project (e.g., 'In Progress', 'Completed').

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task within a project.

assignedTo.description: Reference to User (Team Member/Editor). (Relationship: User 1:N Task)

status.description: Current status of the task (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

Chat.title: Chat
Chat.description: Represents a chat room for a project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat room.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message within a chat.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the message was sent.

fileURL.description: URL of any file attached to the message.

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a file uploaded to a project.

uploadedBy.description: Reference to User who uploaded the file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

fileURL.description: URL of the file in Firebase Storage.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership for user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores project data. Includes the 'clientId' for relationship to the client. Members map should be added to support collaborative editing (denormalized).

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

definition.description: Stores tasks related to a specific project. Inherits project-level access control. Includes 'assignedTo' for user assignment.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the task.

definition.description: Stores a single chat document associated with the project. This path enforces a 1:1 relationship between projects and chats.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

definition.description: Stores messages within a project's chat. Includes 'senderId' for identifying the message sender.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Stores file metadata for files uploaded to a project. Includes 'uploadedBy' to track the uploader and requires a denormalized 'members' map from the project to control access.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the file.

definition.description: Documents in this collection represent admin users. The existence of a document for a given user ID indicates admin privileges. This is existence-based role assignment.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. The core strategy revolves around denormalization and structural segregation to simplify security rules and improve data access patterns.

1.  **Authorization Independence:**  The structure uses path-based ownership and membership maps to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. User-owned data is stored under `/users/{userId}`. Collaborative data (e.g., files) includes denormalized `members` maps, enabling authorization without needing to fetch parent documents.

2.  **Structural Segregation:** Each collection is designed to hold documents with similar security requirements.  For example, project-related data is segregated under `/projects/{projectId}`, ensuring that all documents within a project share the same access control rules.

3.  **Access Modeling:**  The structure consistently applies access modeling patterns:
    *   **Private Data:** User profiles are stored under `/users/{userId}`, providing a clear path-based ownership model.
    *   **Collaborative Data:** The membership map pattern (`members: {uid1: 'role', uid2: 'role'}`) is used for projects and files, allowing for collaborative access control.
    *   **Hierarchical Paths:** Nested data (e.g., tasks within projects, messages within chats) follows a hierarchical path structure, reinforcing ownership and access control.

4.  **QAPs Support:** The structure supports secure `list` operations by ensuring that collections contain homogeneous data with consistent security requirements. Path-based ownership and membership maps enable efficient filtering based on user roles and permissions, preventing the need for client-side filtering.

5.  **Invariants:**  The structure supports data integrity by enforcing ownership (e.g., `clientId` in `/projects/{projectId}`), timestamps (e.g., `createdAt` in `/projects/{projectId}`), and denormalized authorization data (e.g., `members` in `/projects/{projectId}/files/{fileId}`). This design guarantees that security rules can enforce these invariants, maintaining the integrity of the application's data.

Example Scenario:
When a user attempts to upload a file to a project, the security rules check if the user is a member of the project using the denormalized `members` map within the file document. This eliminates the need to fetch the project document to verify membership, enabling atomic operations and simplifying the rules.
